sources.religion: Pew Research Center — Global Religious Landscape 2010–2020
sources.population: World Bank — Population, total (SP.POP.TOTL), annual 1960–2024; Pew 2010/2020 anchors as fallback

practices.name: Madres de Plaza de Mayo
practices.desc: Mothers who marched weekly for the 'disappeared', turning private grief into a durable demand for truth and accountability.

practices.name: Trials of the Juntas
practices.desc: Landmark domestic prosecutions of dictatorship leaders — a rare early model of holding a regime legally to account.

practices.name: Welcome to / Acknowledgement of Country
practices.desc: The formal recognition of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander custodianship that now opens public gatherings nationwide.

practices.name: National Sorry Day
practices.desc: Annual remembrance of the Stolen Generations, marking the 2008 national apology for forced child removals.

practices.name: Srebrenica–Potočari Memorial
practices.desc: The cemetery and memorial centre where the 1995 genocide's victims are identified, named and reburied each July.

practices.name: Interethnic Dialogue Initiatives
practices.desc: Grassroots projects rebuilding trust between Bosniak, Croat and Serb communities across the entity line.

practices.name: Truth & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Documented the residential-school system and its survivors, issuing 94 Calls to Action toward Indigenous reconciliation.

practices.name: Healing Circles
practices.desc: First Nations sentencing and healing circles centring community, accountability and the restoration of relationship.

practices.name: Museum of Memory & Human Rights
practices.desc: A national museum documenting Pinochet-era repression, ensuring the dictatorship's victims remain part of public memory.

practices.name: Rettig & Valech Commissions
practices.desc: Truth commissions that officially recorded killings, disappearances and torture, grounding later reparations.

practices.name: Community Reintegration of Ex-Combatants
practices.desc: Local cleansing rituals and church-led programs reintegrating former child soldiers into their villages.

practices.name: Truth Commission (CEV)
practices.desc: Part of the 2016 peace accord; gathered testimony from victims of a half-century armed conflict to establish a shared record.

practices.name: Special Jurisdiction for Peace
practices.desc: A restorative court trading reduced sentences for truth-telling and reparative work by former combatants.

practices.name: Vergangenheitsaufarbeitung
practices.desc: The sustained public process of confronting Nazi-era guilt through memorials, education and reparations — a model of national reckoning.

practices.name: Stolpersteine
practices.desc: Brass 'stumbling stones' set in pavements outside victims' former homes, returning individual names to the sites of deportation.

practices.name: Historical Memory Movement
practices.desc: Civil-society exhumations and the Democratic Memory Law confronting Franco-era repression after decades of silence.

practices.name: Shimglina Elder Mediation
practices.desc: Respected elders mediating disputes and reconciliation between families and communities through negotiated settlement.

practices.name: National Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Examined abuses under past military governments, contributing to one of West Africa's steadiest democracies.

practices.name: Historical Clarification Commission
practices.desc: UN-backed truth commission documenting the genocide against Maya communities during the civil war.

practices.name: Maya Spirituality & Memory
practices.desc: Ceremonies of exhumation and reburial restoring dignity to the disappeared and reweaving community after mass violence.

practices.name: Aceh Peace Process
practices.desc: The Helsinki accord ending the Aceh conflict, with a later provincial truth and reconciliation commission.

practices.name: Good Friday Agreement
practices.desc: The power-sharing settlement that ended most of the Troubles through consent, parity of esteem and decommissioning.

practices.name: Cross-Community Peace-Building
practices.desc: Integrated education and shared-space projects working across the Catholic–Protestant divide.

practices.name: Hiroshima Peace Memorial
practices.desc: The Peace Memorial Park and Museum at the atomic-bomb hypocentre, anchoring a global anti-nuclear ethic.

practices.name: Article 9 Pacifism
practices.desc: The constitutional renunciation of war that has shaped post-war Japanese civic identity.

practices.name: Khmer Rouge Tribunal (ECCC)
practices.desc: A hybrid UN-Cambodian court that convicted senior Khmer Rouge leaders of genocide and crimes against humanity.

practices.name: Buddhist Mindfulness & Reconciliation
practices.desc: Monastic-led ceremonies and dialogue helping survivors and former cadres share the same villages again.

practices.name: Truth & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Investigated colonial-era and Cold-War massacres, including the Jeju 4·3 and Bodo League killings.

practices.name: Jeju 4·3 Peace Park
practices.desc: A memorial and archive for the 1948–49 uprising's victims, paired with a state apology.

practices.name: Truth & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Documented atrocities across two civil wars, with public hearings broadcast nationally.

practices.name: Palava Hut Dialogue
practices.desc: Traditional community gatherings adapted for grassroots reconciliation between victims and perpetrators.

practices.name: Equity & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: The Arab world's first truth commission, addressing 'years of lead' disappearances and detention.

geography.languages: Afrikaans, German, English, Herero, Khoekhoe, Kwangali, Lozi, Ndonga, Tswana

practices.name: Oputa Panel
practices.desc: A truth commission examining human-rights abuses under successive military regimes.

practices.name: Interfaith Mediation Centre
practices.desc: Christian–Muslim peace work, co-led by a pastor and an imam, de-escalating communal violence in the Middle Belt.

practices.name: Transitional Justice Commissions
practices.desc: Truth-seeking and disappearances commissions established after the 1996–2006 civil war.

geography.languages: English, Māori, New Zealand Sign Language

practices.name: Truth & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Documented two decades of internal conflict, centring the Quechua-speaking highland communities who bore most of the loss.

practices.name: Yuyanapaq Photographic Memory
practices.desc: A national photo archive ('to remember') making the conflict visible to a public that had looked away.

practices.name: Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial
practices.desc: The preserved camp and museum that has become the world's principal site of Holocaust remembrance and education.

practices.name: Carnation Revolution Memory
practices.desc: The near-bloodless revolution, remembered each April, that ended dictatorship and Portugal's colonial wars.

practices.name: Sulha Reconciliation
practices.desc: A traditional Arab mediation ritual restoring honour and relationship between families after harm.

practices.name: Gacaca Courts
practices.desc: Community courts that tried over a million genocide cases through public testimony, confession and locally negotiated reparation.

practices.name: Umuganda
practices.desc: Nationwide community-work mornings rebuilding shared infrastructure and social trust between neighbours across former divides.

practices.name: Truth & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Recorded the civil war's atrocities alongside a Special Court prosecuting those who bore greatest responsibility.

practices.name: Fambul Tok
practices.desc: 'Family Talk' — village bonfire ceremonies of confession and forgiveness rooted in local tradition.

practices.name: CAVR Reception & Reconciliation
practices.desc: Community reconciliation procedures reintegrating low-level offenders through acts of restitution.

practices.name: Nahe Biti (Spreading the Mat)
practices.desc: A traditional ritual of laying out a mat to mediate disputes and restore community harmony.

practices.name: Holodomor Remembrance
practices.desc: National memory of the 1932–33 famine, recognised as genocide, kept through museums and a day of remembrance.

practices.name: Restorative Justice in Schools
practices.desc: Accountability circles replacing punitive suspension, drawing on Indigenous and faith-based dialogue traditions.

practices.name: Equal Justice Initiative Memorials
practices.desc: The National Memorial for Peace and Justice documents racial-terror lynchings, making a buried history publicly legible.

geography.languages: Afrikaans, English, Southern Ndebele, Northern Sotho, Southern Sotho, Swazi, Tswana, Tsonga, Venda, Xhosa, Zulu

practices.name: Truth & Reconciliation Commission
practices.desc: Public hearings granting amnesty in exchange for full disclosure, prioritising restorative truth over retributive prosecution.

practices.name: Ubuntu
practices.desc: An ethic of shared humanity — 'I am because we are' — invoked throughout the transition to anchor reconciliation in relationship.

geography.languages: Chibarwe, English, Kalanga, Khoisan, Ndau, Northern Ndebele, Chewa, Shona, Sotho, Tonga, Tswana, Tsonga, Venda, Xhosa, Zimbabwean Sign Language
